mnemonics.title: Cache Miss Types
mnemonics.content: CCC - Cold, Capacity, Conflict: Three types of cache misses to remember - Compulsory (cold start), Capacity (too small), Conflict (too many mapping to same place)

mnemonics.title: Locality Principles
mnemonics.content: TEMPO - Temporal Early Means Past Operations: Temporal = access again soon; Spatial = access nearby soon. Think 'Time' for temporal.

mnemonics.title: Write Policies
mnemonics.content: WTF - Write Through is Fast? No, Write Through For consistency; Write Back For speed: Write-through ensures consistency but slower; Write-back is faster but complex.

acronyms.usage: AMAT = Hit time + (Miss rate × Miss penalty) - measures overall memory system performance
Replacement policy that evicts the block not used for the longest time
Replacement policy that evicts the oldest block in cache
Critical metric for real-time embedded systems with caches
Performance metric affected by cache efficiency: CPI = Base CPI + (Memory stall cycles per instruction)
Component representing memory stall cycles in performance calculation

visualTips.visualization: Draw a cache as a table with rows (lines) and columns (ways for set-associative). Tag is like an ID, index selects the row, offset selects byte within block.

Memory address = Tag | Index | Offset. Tag uniquely identifies block, Index selects set/line, Offset selects byte within block. Example: 32-bit address with 8KB cache, 64B blocks, 4-way set-assoc = Tag(20) | Index(6) | Offset(6).
Start → Check Cache → Hit? → Yes→ Return Data / No→ Check Lower Level → Return & Load → End. Draw as decision tree showing the path taken on hit vs miss.
Draw as pyramid: CPU → L1 (small, fast) → L2 (larger, slower) → Main Memory (largest, slowest). Arrow thickness represents data flow width.

analogies.analogy: Main memory is the library stacks (far away, slow), cache is the front desk (nearby, fast). When you need a book, check desk first (cache hit) - if not there, go to stacks (cache miss) and bring back multiple books for future use.

analogies.concept: Cache Miss Penalty as Shopping Trip
analogies.analogy: Think of cache miss like forgetting something at home: you must make a special trip (penalty) to get it, wasting time. Better to keep frequently needed items at home (cache).
Direct-mapped is like one parking spot per car (rigid, conflicts); Fully-associative is like any empty spot (flexible, hard to find); Set-associative is like a row of spots (compromise).
